content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
July 19,
1993
Leza Davis
Office of Senator Newton R. Russell
California State Senate
State Capitol,
Room 5061
Sacramento,
CA
95814
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-93-278
Dear Ns.
Davis:
I
am writing to confirm our telephone conversation on
July 7,
1993 wherein we discussed your request for advice
regarding the gift provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).
1
Your letter of July 14,
1993 accurately reflects our
conversation wherein I informed you that Senator Russell’s receipt
of complimentary registration in the amount of $350 to a mediation
training by the Los Angeles County Bar Association would not be
considered a gift.
Free admission to an informational conference
or seminar would be deemed “informational material” if it is
provided to the official for the purpose of assisting him in the
performance of his official duties.
(Regulation 18942.1(a),
copy
enclosed.)
Under Section 82028(b) (1),
“informational material” is
excepted from the definition of a “gift.”
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000 et g.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—93—278
Page
2
I trust this answers your question.
Sincerely,
Jeff Marschner
General
By:
JM:JS:a]c
el,
Legal Division
Enclosure

sections.conclusion: Free admission to an informational conference or seminar would be deemed "informational material" if it is provided to the official for the purpose of assisting him in the performance of his official duties. Under Section 82028(b)(1), "informational material" is excepted from the definition of a "gift."
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a California State Senator's receipt of complimentary registration ($350 value) to a mediation training seminar offered by the Los Angeles County Bar Association constitutes a reportable gift under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The complimentary registration to the mediation training seminar does not constitute a gift because it qualifies as "informational material" under the Political Reform Act, which is exempted from the gift definition when provided to assist the official in performing his duties.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document confirms telephone conversation; no formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections present; substantive legal analysis provided in body of letter; advising letter with factual basis clearly stated.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a California State Senator's receipt of complimentary registration ($350 value) to a mediation training seminar offered by the Los Angeles County Bar Association constitutes a reportable gift under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Free admission to an informational conference or seminar would be deemed "informational material" if it is provided to the official for the purpose of assisting him in the performance of his official duties. Under Section 82028(b)(1), "informational material" is excepted from the definition of a "gift."

Russell California State Senate State Capitol, Room 5061 Sacramento, CA 95814 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A-93-278 Dear Ns. Davis: I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation on July 7, 1993 wherein we discussed your request for advice regarding the gift provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”). 1 Your letter of July 14, 1993 accurately reflects our conversation wherein I informed you that Senator Russell’s receipt of complimentary registration in the amount of $350 to a mediation training by the Los Angeles County Bar Association would not be considered a gift. Free admission to an informational conference or seminar would be deemed “informational material” if it is provided to the official for the purpose of assisting him in the performance of his official duties. (Regulation 18942.1(a), copy enclosed.) Under Section 82028(b) (1), “informational material” is excepted from the definition of a “gift.” 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000 et g. embedding.summary: FPPC advises that Senator Russell's receipt of complimentary registration ($350 value) to a Los Angeles County Bar Association mediation training seminar does not constitute a reportable gift, as it qualifies as exempt "informational material" under the Political Reform Act.
